verses.greek: Ἰάκωβος θεοῦ καὶ κυρίου Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ δοῦλος ταῖς δώδεκα φυλαῖς ταῖς ἐν τῇ διασπορᾷ χαίρειν.
verses.mistral: James, a servant of God and the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes scattered among the nations: Greetings.
verses.kjv: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are scattered abroad, greeting.

verses.greek: Πᾶσαν χαρὰν ἡγήσασθε, ἀδελφοί μου, ὅταν πειρασμοῖς περιπέσητε ποικίλοις,
verses.mistral: All joy to you, my brothers, when you are enmeshed in various trials.
verses.kjv: My brethren, count it all joy when ye fall into divers temptations;

words.gloss: I fall into the midst of

verses.greek: γινώσκοντες ὅτι τὸ δοκίμιον ὑμῶν τῆς πίστεως κατεργάζεται ὑπομονήν·
verses.mistral: Your trial is testing the genuineness of your faith.
verses.kjv: Knowing this, that the trying of your faith worketh patience.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ ὑπομονὴ ἔργον τέλειον ἐχέτω, ἵνα ἦτε τέλειοι καὶ ὁλόκληροι, ἐν μηδενὶ λειπόμενοι.
verses.kjv: But let patience have her perfect work, that ye may be perfect and entire, wanting nothing.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I am wanting, am left behind

verses.greek: Εἰ δέ τις ὑμῶν λείπεται σοφίας, αἰτείτω παρὰ τοῦ διδόντος θεοῦ πᾶσιν ἁπλῶς καὶ ⸀μὴ ὀνειδίζοντος, καὶ δοθήσεται αὐτῷ·
verses.mistral: If anyone among you lacks wisdom, let him ask from the One giving to all generously and without rebuke, and it will be given to him.
verses.kjv: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God, that giveth to all men liberally, and upbraideth not; and it shall be given him.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I am wanting, am left behind

verses.greek: αἰτείτω δὲ ἐν πίστει, μηδὲν διακρινόμενος, ὁ γὰρ διακρινόμενος ἔοικεν κλύδωνι θαλάσσης ἀνεμιζομένῳ καὶ ῥιπιζομένῳ·
verses.mistral: Ask in faith, without doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind.
verses.kjv: But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: μὴ γὰρ οἰέσθω ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἐκεῖνος ὅτι λήμψεταί τι παρὰ τοῦ κυρίου
verses.mistral: Does not the man suppose that he will receive something from the Lord?
verses.kjv: For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord.

verses.greek: ἀνὴρ δίψυχος, ἀκατάστατος ἐν πάσαις ταῖς ὁδοῖς αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.
verses.kjv: A double minded man is unstable in all his ways.

verses.greek: Καυχάσθω δὲ ὁ ἀδελφὸς ὁ ταπεινὸς ἐν τῷ ὕψει αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Exalt, [therefore], the brother who is humble in his high position.
verses.kjv: Let the brother of low degree rejoice in that he is exalted:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ πλούσιος ἐν τῇ ταπεινώσει αὐτοῦ, ὅτι ὡς ἄνθος χόρτου παρελεύσεται.
verses.mistral: The rich in his humble state will fade away, like a flower of the field.
verses.kjv: But the rich, in that he is made low: because as the flower of the grass he shall pass away.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἀνέτειλεν γὰρ ὁ ἥλιος σὺν τῷ καύσωνι καὶ ἐξήρανεν τὸν χόρτον, καὶ τὸ ἄνθος αὐτοῦ ἐξέπεσεν καὶ ἡ εὐπρέπεια τοῦ προσώπου αὐτοῦ ἀπώλετο· οὕτως καὶ ὁ πλούσιος ἐν ταῖς πορείαις αὐτοῦ μαρανθήσεται.
verses.mistral: The sun, with its scorching heat, dries up the grass; its flower falls and its beautiful appearance is changed. So it is with the wealthy man in his lifetime—he will fade away.
verses.kjv: For the sun is no sooner risen with a burning heat, but it withereth the grass, and the flower thereof falleth, and the grace of the fashion of it perisheth: so also shall the rich man fade away in his ways.

words.gloss: I make to rise, I rise, shine
I fall out, fall off, fall away, fade away, wither away

verses.greek: Μακάριος ἀνὴρ ὃς ὑπομένει πειρασμόν, ὅτι δόκιμος γενόμενος λήμψεται τὸν στέφανον τῆς ζωῆς, ὃν ⸀ἐπηγγείλατο τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Blessed is the man who perseveres under trial, because when he has been tested, he will receive the crown of life that the Lord promised to those who love him.
verses.kjv: Blessed is the man that endureth temptation: for when he is tried, he shall receive the crown of life, which the Lord hath promised to them that love him.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: μηδεὶς πειραζόμενος λεγέτω ὅτι Ἀπὸ θεοῦ πειράζομαι· ὁ γὰρ θεὸς ἀπείραστός ἐστιν κακῶν, πειράζει δὲ αὐτὸς οὐδένα.
verses.mistral: Nobody, being tempted, should say that he is tempted by God; for God cannot be tempted with evil, and He Himself tempts no one.
verses.kjv: Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God: for God cannot be tempted with evil, neither tempteth he any man:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἕκαστος δὲ πειράζεται ὑπὸ τῆς ἰδίας ἐπιθυμίας ἐξελκόμενος καὶ δελεαζόμενος·
verses.mistral: Each one is tempted by his own desire, being drawn away and enticed by it.
verses.kjv: But every man is tempted, when he is drawn away of his own lust, and enticed.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I allure, entice by a bait

verses.greek: εἶτα ἡ ἐπιθυμία συλλαβοῦσα τίκτει ἁμαρτίαν, ἡ δὲ ἁμαρτία ἀποτελεσθεῖσα ἀποκύει θάνατον.
verses.mistral: Desire gives birth to sin, and sin gives birth to death.
verses.kjv: Then when lust hath conceived, it bringeth forth sin: and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I complete, accomplish, form fully, perfect
I bring forth, give birth to

verses.greek: μὴ πλανᾶσθε, ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοί.
verses.mistral: Do not be deceived, my beloved brothers.
verses.kjv: Do not err, my beloved brethren.

verses.greek: Πᾶσα δόσις ἀγαθὴ καὶ πᾶν δώρημα τέλειον ἄνωθέν ἐστιν, καταβαῖνον ἀπὸ τοῦ πατρὸς τῶν φώτων, παρ’ ᾧ οὐκ ἔνι παραλλαγὴ ἢ τροπῆς ἀποσκίασμα.
verses.mistral: Every generous act and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom there is no variation or shadow due to change.
verses.kjv: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, and cometh down from the Father of lights, with whom is no variableness, neither shadow of turning.

words.gloss: from above, from the beginning, again

verses.greek: βουληθεὶς ἀπεκύησεν ἡμᾶς λόγῳ ἀληθείας, εἰς τὸ εἶναι ἡμᾶς ἀπαρχήν τινα τῶν αὐτοῦ κτισμάτων.
verses.mistral: Desired and brought forth us by truth, to be a firstfruit of his creatures.
verses.kjv: Of his own will begat he us with the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures.

words.gloss: I bring forth, give birth to

verses.greek: ⸀Ἴστε, ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοί. ἔστω ⸀δὲ πᾶς ἄνθρωπος ταχὺς εἰς τὸ ἀκοῦσαι, βραδὺς εἰς τὸ λαλῆσαι, βραδὺς εἰς ὀργήν,
verses.mistral: My beloved brethren, let every person be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to anger.
verses.kjv: Wherefore, my beloved brethren, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὀργὴ γὰρ ἀνδρὸς δικαιοσύνην θεοῦ ⸂οὐκ ἐργάζεται⸃.
verses.mistral: Anger does not produce the righteousness of God.
verses.kjv: For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.

verses.greek: διὸ ἀποθέμενοι πᾶσαν ῥυπαρίαν καὶ περισσείαν κακίας ἐν πραΰτητι δέξασθε τὸν ἔμφυτον λόγον τὸν δυνάμενον σῶσαι τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: Laying aside all bitterness, anger, wrath, and malice, welcome one another as you would welcome the Messenger of righteousness. This message is powerful enough to save your souls.
verses.kjv: Wherefore lay apart all filthiness and superfluity of naughtiness, and receive with meekness the engrafted word, which is able to save your souls.

words.gloss: I lay off or aside, renounce

verses.greek: Γίνεσθε δὲ ποιηταὶ λόγου καὶ μὴ ⸂ἀκροαταὶ μόνον⸃ παραλογιζόμενοι ἑαυτούς.
verses.mistral: Become doers of the word, and not hearers only deceiving yourselves.
verses.kjv: But be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own selves.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὅτι εἴ τις ἀκροατὴς λόγου ἐστὶν καὶ οὐ ποιητής, οὗτος ἔοικεν ἀνδρὶ κατανοοῦντι τὸ πρόσωπον τῆς γενέσεως αὐτοῦ ἐν ἐσόπτρῳ,
verses.mistral: Appears to be a man looking in a mirror Please note that this is a machine translation and may not be fully accurate. For the most precise translation of ancient texts, it's always best to consult a classical scholar or use specialized resources. In this case, the phrase " " can be translated as "he looks like one who catches a glimpse of his own birth in a mirror," which is an idiomatic expression that James uses to illustrate the point made in the previous verse.
verses.kjv: For if any be a hearer of the word, and not a doer, he is like unto a man beholding his natural face in a glass:

verses.greek: κατενόησεν γὰρ ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀπελήλυθεν καὶ εὐθέως ἐπελάθετο ὁποῖος ἦν.
verses.mistral: Forgot himself and immediately fell away, being the man he was.
verses.kjv: For he beholdeth himself, and goeth his way, and straightway forgetteth what manner of man he was.

words.gloss: I come or go away from, depart, return

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ παρακύψας εἰς νόμον τέλειον τὸν τῆς ἐλευθερίας καὶ παραμείνας, ⸀οὐκ ἀκροατὴς ἐπιλησμονῆς γενόμενος ἀλλὰ ποιητὴς ἔργου, οὗτος μακάριος ἐν τῇ ποιήσει αὐτοῦ ἔσται.
verses.mistral: This man, who has looked into the perfect law of freedom and continues, not becoming a hearer of the word but a doer of deeds, this one will be blessed in his work.
verses.kjv: But whoso looketh into the perfect law of liberty, and continueth therein, he being not a forgetful hearer, but a doer of the work, this man shall be blessed in his deed.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I stoop, peer in, look down
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Εἴ τις δοκεῖ θρησκὸς ⸀εἶναι μὴ χαλιναγωγῶν γλῶσσαν ⸀αὐτοῦ ἀλλὰ ἀπατῶν καρδίαν ⸀αὐτοῦ, τούτου μάταιος ἡ θρησκεία.
verses.mistral: Deceitful is the religion of one who claims to be religious but deceives his own heart.
verses.kjv: If any man among you seem to be religious, and bridleth not his tongue, but deceiveth his own heart, this man's religion is vain.

words.gloss: I think, seem, appear, it seems

verses.greek: θρησκεία καθαρὰ καὶ ἀμίαντος παρὰ ⸀τῷ θεῷ καὶ πατρὶ αὕτη ἐστίν, ἐπισκέπτεσθαι ὀρφανοὺς καὶ χήρας ἐν τῇ θλίψει αὐτῶν, ἄσπιλον ἑαυτὸν τηρεῖν ἀπὸ τοῦ κόσμου.
verses.mistral: Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their trouble, and to keep oneself unspotted from the world.
verses.kjv: Pure religion and undefiled before God and the Father is this, To visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep himself unspotted from the world.

words.gloss: I look upon, visit, look out, select

verses.greek: Ἀδελφοί μου, μὴ ἐν προσωπολημψίαις ἔχετε τὴν πίστιν τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ τῆς δόξης;
verses.mistral: Have no arrogance, brothers, regarding the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: My brethren, have not the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord of glory, with respect of persons.

verses.greek: ἐὰν γὰρ εἰσέλθῃ ⸀εἰς συναγωγὴν ὑμῶν ἀνὴρ χρυσοδακτύλιος ἐν ἐσθῆτι λαμπρᾷ, εἰσέλθῃ δὲ καὶ πτωχὸς ἐν ῥυπαρᾷ ἐσθῆτι,
verses.mistral: If a man wearing golden rings and fine clothing comes into your assembly, yet a poor man in shabby clothes also comes in,
verses.kjv: For if there come unto your assembly a man with a gold ring, in goodly apparel, and there come in also a poor man in vile raiment;

words.gloss: I go in, come in, enter
I go in, come in, enter
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸂ἐπιβλέψητε δὲ⸃ ἐπὶ τὸν φοροῦντα τὴν ἐσθῆτα τὴν λαμπρὰν καὶ ⸀εἴπητε· Σὺ κάθου ὧδε καλῶς, καὶ τῷ πτωχῷ εἴπητε· Σὺ στῆθι ⸂ἢ κάθου ἐκεῖ⸃ ὑπὸ τὸ ὑποπόδιόν μου,
verses.mistral: Look at the one wearing the fine clothing and say, "Sit here in a good place," and to the poor man say, "Stand there [or sit] by my footstool."
verses.kjv: And ye have respect to him that weareth the gay clothing, and say unto him, Sit thou here in a good place; and say to the poor, Stand thou there, or sit here under my footstool:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ⸀οὐ διεκρίθητε ἐν ἑαυτοῖς καὶ ἐγένεσθε κριταὶ διαλογισμῶν πονηρῶν;
verses.mistral: You did not discriminate among yourselves and became judges of wicked discussions.
verses.kjv: Are ye not then partial in yourselves, and are become judges of evil thoughts?

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἀκούσατε, ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοί. οὐχ ὁ θεὸς ἐξελέξατο τοὺς πτωχοὺς ⸂τῷ κόσμῳ⸃ πλουσίους ἐν πίστει καὶ κληρονόμους τῆς βασιλείας ἧς ἐπηγγείλατο τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν αὐτόν;
verses.mistral: Hear, my beloved brethren. Has not God chosen the poor in the world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom which He promised to those who love Him?
verses.kjv: Hearken, my beloved brethren, Hath not God chosen the poor of this world rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom which he hath promised to them that love him?

verses.greek: ὑμεῖς δὲ ἠτιμάσατε τὸν πτωχόν. οὐχ οἱ πλούσιοι καταδυναστεύουσιν ὑμῶν, καὶ αὐτοὶ ἕλκουσιν ὑμᾶς εἰς κριτήρια;
verses.mistral: You have shown favor to the poor. Do not the rich oppress you, and drag you into the courts of law?
verses.kjv: But ye have despised the poor. Do not rich men oppress you, and draw you before the judgment seats?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὐκ αὐτοὶ βλασφημοῦσιν τὸ καλὸν ὄνομα τὸ ἐπικληθὲν ἐφ’ ὑμᾶς;
verses.mistral: They do not blaspheme the good name that is called upon you.
verses.kjv: Do not they blaspheme that worthy name by the which ye are called?

words.gloss: I call by an additional name, appeal to
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: Εἰ μέντοι νόμον τελεῖτε βασιλικὸν κατὰ τὴν γραφήν Ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν, καλῶς ποιεῖτε·
verses.mistral: If you fulfill the royal law according to the scripture, "You shall love your neighbor as yourself," you do well.
verses.kjv: If ye fulfil the royal law according to the scripture, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself, ye do well:

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ προσωπολημπτεῖτε, ἁμαρτίαν ἐργάζεσθε, ἐλεγχόμενοι ὑπὸ τοῦ νόμου ὡς παραβάται.
verses.mistral: If you show partiality, you commit sin, being condemned by the law as transgressors.
verses.kjv: But if ye have respect to persons, ye commit sin, and are convinced of the law as transgressors.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὅστις γὰρ ὅλον τὸν νόμον ⸂τηρήσῃ, πταίσῃ⸃ δὲ ἐν ἑνί, γέγονεν πάντων ἔνοχος.
verses.mistral: Whoever keeps the whole law and yet stumbles in one point, has become accountable for all of it.
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall keep the whole law, and yet offend in one point, he is guilty of all.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ εἰπών Μὴ ⸀μοιχεύσῃς εἶπεν καί Μὴ ⸀φονεύσῃς· εἰ δὲ οὐ ⸂μοιχεύεις φονεύεις⸃ δέ, γέγονας παραβάτης νόμου.
verses.mistral: He who says, 'Do not commit adultery,' also says, 'Do not murder.' If you do not commit adultery but murder, you have become a lawbreaker.
verses.kjv: For he that said, Do not commit adultery, said also, Do not kill. Now if thou commit no adultery, yet if thou kill, thou art become a transgressor of the law.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: οὕτως λαλεῖτε καὶ οὕτως ποιεῖτε ὡς διὰ νόμου ἐλευθερίας μέλλοντες κρίνεσθαι.
verses.mistral: So also you speak and so you do as if for the sake of freedom through the law; however, you are enslaved by it.
verses.kjv: So speak ye, and so do, as they that shall be judged by the law of liberty.

verses.greek: ἡ γὰρ κρίσις ἀνέλεος τῷ μὴ ποιήσαντι ἔλεος· κατακαυχᾶται ⸀ἔλεος κρίσεως.
verses.mistral: The judgment is unmerciful to the one who does not show mercy; mercy triumphs over judgment.
verses.kjv: For he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath shewed no mercy; and mercy rejoiceth against judgment.

verses.greek: ⸀Τί ὄφελος, ἀδελφοί μου, ἐὰν πίστιν λέγῃ τις ἔχειν ἔργα δὲ μὴ ἔχῃ; μὴ δύναται ἡ πίστις σῶσαι αὐτόν;
verses.mistral: Faith without works is useless, my brothers. Can faith save one who has no works?
verses.kjv: What doth it profit, my brethren, though a man say he hath faith, and have not works? can faith save him?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸀ἐὰν ἀδελφὸς ἢ ἀδελφὴ γυμνοὶ ὑπάρχωσιν καὶ ⸀λειπόμενοι τῆς ἐφημέρου τροφῆς,
verses.mistral: If a brother or sister lack clothing and daily food,
verses.kjv: If a brother or sister be naked, and destitute of daily food,

words.gloss: I am, exist, am in possession
I am wanting, am left behind

verses.greek: εἴπῃ δέ τις αὐτοῖς ἐξ ὑμῶν· Ὑπάγετε ἐν εἰρήνῃ, θερμαίνεσθε καὶ χορτάζεσθε, μὴ δῶτε δὲ αὐτοῖς τὰ ἐπιτήδεια τοῦ σώματος, ⸀τί ὄφελος;
verses.mistral: Provide them with what is necessary for the body, but why ask?
verses.kjv: And one of you say unto them, Depart in peace, be ye warmed and filled; notwithstanding ye give them not those things which are needful to the body; what doth it profit?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ἡ πίστις, ἐὰν μὴ ⸂ἔχῃ ἔργα⸃, νεκρά ἐστιν καθ’ ἑαυτήν.
verses.mistral: Faith, if it does not have works, is dead to itself.
verses.kjv: Even so faith, if it hath not works, is dead, being alone.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: Ἀλλ’ ἐρεῖ τις· Σὺ πίστιν ἔχεις κἀγὼ ἔργα ἔχω. δεῖξόν μοι τὴν πίστιν σου ⸀χωρὶς τῶν ⸀ἔργων, κἀγώ ⸂σοι δείξω⸃ ἐκ τῶν ἔργων μου τὴν ⸀πίστιν.
verses.mistral: You will say to me, "You have faith and I have works." Show me your faith apart from your works, and I will show you my faith by my works.
verses.kjv: Yea, a man may say, Thou hast faith, and I have works: shew me thy faith without thy works, and I will shew thee my faith by my works.

verses.greek: σὺ πιστεύεις ὅτι ⸂εἷς ἐστιν ὁ θεός⸃; καλῶς ποιεῖς· καὶ τὰ δαιμόνια πιστεύουσιν καὶ φρίσσουσιν.
verses.mistral: You believe that there is one God; you do well. Even the demons believe and shudder.
verses.kjv: Thou believest that there is one God; thou doest well: the devils also believe, and tremble.

verses.greek: θέλεις δὲ γνῶναι, ὦ ἄνθρωπε κενέ, ὅτι ἡ πίστις χωρὶς τῶν ἔργων ⸀ἀργή ἐστιν;
verses.mistral: Desires thou to know, O man, neither art thou, that faith without works is dead?
verses.kjv: But wilt thou know, O vain man, that faith without works is dead?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: Ἀβραὰμ ὁ πατὴρ ἡμῶν οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων ἐδικαιώθη, ἀνενέγκας Ἰσαὰκ τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον;
verses.mistral: Abraham our father was not declared righteous by works, offering up Isaac his son on the altar?
verses.kjv: Was not Abraham our father justified by works, when he had offered Isaac his son upon the altar?

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify
I carry up, lead up, offer up
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: βλέπεις ὅτι ἡ πίστις συνήργει τοῖς ἔργοις αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐκ τῶν ἔργων ἡ πίστις ἐτελειώθη,
verses.mistral: Faith works together with works, and by works faith is completed.
verses.kjv: Seest thou how faith wrought with his works, and by works was faith made perfect?

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπληρώθη ἡ γραφὴ ἡ λέγουσα· Ἐπίστευσεν δὲ Ἀβραὰμ τῷ θεῷ, καὶ ἐλογίσθη αὐτῷ εἰς δικαιοσύνην, καὶ φίλος θεοῦ ἐκλήθη.
verses.mistral: And was fulfilled the scripture which says: "Abraham believed God, and he was accounted to him for righteousness, and he was called the friend of God."
verses.kjv: And the scripture was fulfilled which saith, Abraham believed God, and it was imputed unto him for righteousness: and he was called the Friend of God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸀ὁρᾶτε ὅτι ἐξ ἔργων δικαιοῦται ἄνθρωπος καὶ οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως μόνον.
verses.mistral: A man is justified by works, and not by faith alone.
verses.kjv: Ye see then how that by works a man is justified, and not by faith only.

words.gloss: I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify

verses.greek: ὁμοίως δὲ καὶ Ῥαὰβ ἡ πόρνη οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων ἐδικαιώθη, ὑποδεξαμένη τοὺς ἀγγέλους καὶ ἑτέρᾳ ὁδῷ ἐκβαλοῦσα;
verses.mistral: The same is not the case with Rahab the prostitute, was she not justified by works when she welcomed the messengers and sent them out another way?
verses.kjv: Likewise also was not Rahab the harlot justified by works, when she had received the messengers, and had sent them out another way?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I make righteous, defend the cause of, justify
I throw, cast, put out, banish, bring forth, produce

verses.greek: ὥσπερ ⸀γὰρ τὸ σῶμα χωρὶς πνεύματος νεκρόν ἐστιν, οὕτως καὶ ἡ πίστις ⸀χωρὶς ἔργων νεκρά ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: The same as a body without a spirit is dead, so faith without deeds is dead.
verses.kjv: For as the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also.

verses.greek: Μὴ πολλοὶ διδάσκαλοι γίνεσθε, ἀδελφοί μου, εἰδότες ὅτι μεῖζον κρίμα λημψόμεθα·
verses.mistral: Not many teachers, my brethren, become such, knowing that a greater judgment we shall receive.
verses.kjv: My brethren, be not many masters, knowing that we shall receive the greater condemnation.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: πολλὰ γὰρ πταίομεν ἅπαντες. εἴ τις ἐν λόγῳ οὐ πταίει, οὗτος τέλειος ἀνήρ, δυνατὸς χαλιναγωγῆσαι καὶ ὅλον τὸ σῶμα.
verses.mistral: We all stumble in many ways. If anyone does not stumble in his speech, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle the whole body.
verses.kjv: For in many things we offend all. If any man offend not in word, the same is a perfect man, and able also to bridle the whole body.

verses.greek: ⸂εἰ δὲ⸃ τῶν ἵππων τοὺς χαλινοὺς εἰς τὰ στόματα βάλλομεν ⸀εἰς τὸ πείθεσθαι αὐτοὺς ἡμῖν, καὶ ὅλον τὸ σῶμα αὐτῶν μετάγομεν.
verses.mistral: If we control the horses' mouths to make them obey us, we can steer the entire body as well.
verses.kjv: Behold, we put bits in the horses' mouths, that they may obey us; and we turn about their whole body.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I cast, throw, rush, put, place, drop
I turn about, change the position of

verses.greek: ἰδοὺ καὶ τὰ πλοῖα, τηλικαῦτα ὄντα καὶ ὑπὸ ⸂ἀνέμων σκληρῶν⸃ ἐλαυνόμενα, μετάγεται ὑπὸ ἐλαχίστου πηδαλίου ὅπου ⸂ἡ ὁρμὴ τοῦ εὐθύνοντος βούλεται⸃·
verses.mistral: And behold, even ships, though they be so great and carried by harsh winds, are turned by the smallest rudder at the will of the steersman.
verses.kjv: Behold also the ships, which though they be so great, and are driven of fierce winds, yet are they turned about with a very small helm, whithersoever the governor listeth.

words.gloss: I turn about, change the position of

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ἡ γλῶσσα μικρὸν μέλος ἐστὶν καὶ ⸂μεγάλα αὐχεῖ⸃. Ἰδοὺ ⸀ἡλίκον πῦρ ἡλίκην ὕλην ἀνάπτει·
verses.mistral: Thus also the tongue is a small member, yet it boasts greatly.
verses.kjv: Even so the tongue is a little member, and boasteth great things. Behold, how great a matter a little fire kindleth!

verses.greek: καὶ ἡ γλῶσσα πῦρ, ὁ κόσμος τῆς ⸀ἀδικίας ἡ γλῶσσα καθίσταται ἐν τοῖς μέλεσιν ἡμῶν, ἡ σπιλοῦσα ὅλον τὸ σῶμα καὶ φλογίζουσα τὸν τροχὸν τῆς γενέσεως καὶ φλογιζομένη ὑπὸ τῆς γεέννης.
verses.mistral: And the tongue is a fire, the world of unrighteousness; the tongue is set among our members, stirring up the whole body, and setting on fire the course of nature, and being set on fire by hell.
verses.kjv: And the tongue is a fire, a world of iniquity: so is the tongue among our members, that it defileth the whole body, and setteth on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire of hell.

verses.greek: πᾶσα γὰρ φύσις θηρίων τε καὶ πετεινῶν ἑρπετῶν τε καὶ ἐναλίων δαμάζεται καὶ δεδάμασται τῇ φύσει τῇ ἀνθρωπίνῃ·
verses.mistral: All flesh is subject to mankind's dominion (The Greek original: "Pasa gar physis therion te kai peteinon herpeton te kai enalion damazetai kai dedamastai tē physei tē anthropini.")
verses.kjv: For every kind of beasts, and of birds, and of serpents, and of things in the sea, is tamed, and hath been tamed of mankind:

verses.greek: τὴν δὲ γλῶσσαν οὐδεὶς ⸂δαμάσαι δύναται ἀνθρώπων⸃· ⸀ἀκατάστατον κακόν, μεστὴ ἰοῦ θανατηφόρου.
verses.mistral: Man cannot tame the tongue; it is unruly and a source of deceit.
verses.kjv: But the tongue can no man tame; it is an unruly evil, full of deadly poison.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐν αὐτῇ εὐλογοῦμεν τὸν ⸀κύριον καὶ πατέρα, καὶ ἐν αὐτῇ καταρώμεθα τοὺς ἀνθρώπους τοὺς καθ’ ὁμοίωσιν θεοῦ γεγονότας·
verses.mistral: Bless in her the Lord and Father, and curse in her the people who are according to God's likeness become.
verses.kjv: Therewith bless we God, even the Father; and therewith curse we men, which are made after the similitude of God.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἐκ τοῦ αὐτοῦ στόματος ἐξέρχεται εὐλογία καὶ κατάρα. οὐ χρή, ἀδελφοί μου, ταῦτα οὕτως γίνεσθαι.
verses.mistral: From the same mouth comes blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things should not be so.
verses.kjv: Out of the same mouth proceedeth blessing and cursing. My brethren, these things ought not so to be.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: μήτι ἡ πηγὴ ἐκ τῆς αὐτῆς ὀπῆς βρύει τὸ γλυκὺ καὶ τὸ πικρόν;
verses.mistral: Why does a spring produce both sweet and bitter water?
verses.kjv: Doth a fountain send forth at the same place sweet water and bitter?

words.gloss: I cause to gush forth, send forth

verses.greek: μὴ δύναται, ἀδελφοί μου, συκῆ ἐλαίας ποιῆσαι ἢ ἄμπελος σῦκα; ⸂οὔτε ἁλυκὸν⸃ γλυκὺ ποιῆσαι ὕδωρ.
verses.mistral: Cannot, my brothers, make a fig tree bear olives, or a vine produce figs. Neither can salt water become sweet.
verses.kjv: Can the fig tree, my brethren, bear olive berries? either a vine, figs? so can no fountain both yield salt water and fresh.

verses.greek: Τίς σοφὸς καὶ ἐπιστήμων ἐν ὑμῖν; δειξάτω ἐκ τῆς καλῆς ἀναστροφῆς τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ ἐν πραΰτητι σοφίας.
verses.mistral: Who among you is wise and understanding? Let him show it by his good life, in deeds done in mercy.
verses.kjv: Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom.

verses.greek: εἰ δὲ ζῆλον πικρὸν ἔχετε καὶ ἐριθείαν ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ ὑμῶν, μὴ κατακαυχᾶσθε καὶ ψεύδεσθε κατὰ τῆς ἀληθείας.
verses.mistral: Harbor bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast about it or lie against the truth.
verses.kjv: But if ye have bitter envying and strife in your hearts, glory not, and lie not against the truth.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔστιν αὕτη ἡ σοφία ἄνωθεν κατερχομένη, ἀλλὰ ἐπίγειος, ψυχική, δαιμονιώδης·
verses.mistral: This is not wisdom from above, but earthly, sensual, demonic.
verses.kjv: This wisdom descendeth not from above, but is earthly, sensual, devilish.

words.gloss: from above, from the beginning, again

verses.greek: ὅπου γὰρ ζῆλος καὶ ἐριθεία, ἐκεῖ ἀκαταστασία καὶ πᾶν φαῦλον πρᾶγμα.
verses.mistral: Where jealousy and strife exist, there is disorder and every evil work.
verses.kjv: For where envying and strife is, there is confusion and every evil work.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ ἄνωθεν σοφία πρῶτον μὲν ἁγνή ἐστιν, ἔπειτα εἰρηνική, ἐπιεικής, εὐπειθής, μεστὴ ἐλέους καὶ καρπῶν ἀγαθῶν, ⸀ἀδιάκριτος, ἀνυπόκριτος·
verses.mistral: The wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, willing to yield, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial and sincere.
verses.kjv: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and easy to be intreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
from above, from the beginning, again

verses.greek: καρπὸς ⸀δὲ δικαιοσύνης ἐν εἰρήνῃ σπείρεται τοῖς ποιοῦσιν εἰρήνην.
verses.mistral: Sown in peace by the seeds of righteousness.
verses.kjv: And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace of them that make peace.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Πόθεν πόλεμοι καὶ ⸀πόθεν μάχαι ἐν ὑμῖν; οὐκ ἐντεῦθεν, ἐκ τῶν ἡδονῶν ὑμῶν τῶν στρατευομένων ἐν τοῖς μέλεσιν ὑμῶν;
verses.mistral: From where do wars and fights among you come? Do they not come from your desires for pleasure that are at war within you?
verses.kjv: From whence come wars and fightings among you? come they not hence, even of your lusts that war in your members?

words.gloss: hence, from this place, on this side and on that

verses.greek: ἐπιθυμεῖτε, καὶ οὐκ ἔχετε· φονεύετε καὶ ζηλοῦτε, καὶ οὐ δύνασθε ἐπιτυχεῖν· μάχεσθε καὶ πολεμεῖτε. οὐκ ἔχετε διὰ τὸ μὴ αἰτεῖσθαι ὑμᾶς·
verses.mistral: You desire, yet you have not; you kill and covet, yet you cannot obtain; you fight and war. You do not have because you do not ask.
verses.kjv: Ye lust, and have not: ye kill, and desire to have, and cannot obtain: ye fight and war, yet ye have not, because ye ask not.

words.gloss: I long for, covet, lust after
I engage in battle, fight, strive

verses.greek: αἰτεῖτε καὶ οὐ λαμβάνετε, διότι κακῶς αἰτεῖσθε, ἵνα ἐν ταῖς ἡδοναῖς ὑμῶν δαπανήσητε.
verses.mistral: Ask and do not receive, because you ask amiss, that you may spend it on your pleasures.
verses.kjv: Ye ask, and receive not, because ye ask amiss, that ye may consume it upon your lusts.

verses.greek: ⸀μοιχαλίδες, οὐκ οἴδατε ὅτι ἡ φιλία τοῦ κόσμου ἔχθρα τοῦ θεοῦ ἐστιν; ὃς ⸀ἐὰν οὖν βουληθῇ φίλος εἶναι τοῦ κόσμου, ἐχθρὸς τοῦ θεοῦ καθίσταται.
verses.mistral: Adulteresses, do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
verses.kjv: Ye adulterers and adulteresses, know ye not that the friendship of the world is enmity with God? whosoever therefore will be a friend of the world is the enemy of God.

verses.greek: ἢ δοκεῖτε ὅτι κενῶς ἡ γραφὴ λέγει· Πρὸς φθόνον ἐπιποθεῖ τὸ πνεῦμα ὃ ⸀κατῴκισεν ἐν ἡμῖν;
verses.mistral: Does it say in the Scripture, "The Spirit that He caused to dwell in us is envied?"
verses.kjv: Do ye think that the scripture saith in vain, The spirit that dwelleth in us lusteth to envy?

words.gloss: I think, seem, appear, it seems
I long for, strain after, desire greatly

verses.greek: μείζονα δὲ δίδωσιν χάριν· διὸ λέγει· Ὁ θεὸς ὑπερηφάνοις ἀντιτάσσεται ταπεινοῖς δὲ δίδωσιν χάριν.
verses.mistral: God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.
verses.kjv: But he giveth more grace. Wherefore he saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὑποτάγητε οὖν τῷ θεῷ· ἀντίστητε δὲ τῷ διαβόλῳ, καὶ φεύξεται ἀφ’ ὑμῶν·
verses.mistral: Submit to God, resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
verses.kjv: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

words.gloss: I take a stand against, oppose, resist
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐγγίσατε τῷ θεῷ, καὶ ⸀ἐγγιεῖ ὑμῖν. καθαρίσατε χεῖρας, ἁμαρτωλοί, καὶ ἁγνίσατε καρδίας, δίψυχοι.
verses.mistral: Draw near to God, and He will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, sinners, and purify your hearts, double-minded.
verses.kjv: Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you. Cleanse your hands, ye sinners; and purify your hearts, ye double minded.

verses.greek: ταλαιπωρήσατε καὶ πενθήσατε καὶ κλαύσατε· ὁ γέλως ὑμῶν εἰς πένθος ⸀μετατραπήτω καὶ ἡ χαρὰ εἰς κατήφειαν·
verses.mistral: Mourn and weep and wail. Let your laughter be turned to mourning and your joy to gloom.
verses.kjv: Be afflicted, and mourn, and weep: let your laughter be turned to mourning, and your joy to heaviness.

verses.greek: ταπεινώθητε ἐνώπιον ⸀κυρίου, καὶ ὑψώσει ὑμᾶς.
verses.mistral: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
verses.kjv: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he shall lift you up.

words.gloss: before the face of, in the presence of

verses.greek: Μὴ καταλαλεῖτε ἀλλήλων, ἀδελφοί· ὁ καταλαλῶν ἀδελφοῦ ⸀ἢ κρίνων τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὐτοῦ καταλαλεῖ νόμου καὶ κρίνει νόμον· εἰ δὲ νόμον κρίνεις, οὐκ εἶ ποιητὴς νόμου ἀλλὰ κριτής.
verses.mistral: Do not speak evil against one another, brethren. He who speaks evil against a brother or judges his brother, speaks evil about the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.
verses.kjv: Speak not evil one of another, brethren. He that speaketh evil of his brother, and judgeth his brother, speaketh evil of the law, and judgeth the law: but if thou judge the law, thou art not a doer of the law, but a judge.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: εἷς ⸀ἐστιν νομοθέτης ⸂καὶ κριτής⸃, ὁ δυνάμενος σῶσαι καὶ ἀπολέσαι· σὺ δὲ τίς εἶ, ⸂ὁ κρίνων⸃ τὸν ⸀πλησίον;
verses.mistral: One is a lawgiver and a judge, one who can save and destroy. But who are you to judge another?
verses.kjv: There is one lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy: who art thou that judgest another?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ἄγε νῦν οἱ λέγοντες· Σήμερον ⸀ἢ αὔριον ⸀πορευσόμεθα εἰς τήνδε τὴν πόλιν καὶ ⸀ποιήσομεν ἐκεῖ ⸀ἐνιαυτὸν καὶ ⸀ἐμπορευσόμεθα καὶ ⸀κερδήσομεν·
verses.mistral: Go now, you who say: Today or tomorrow we will go to this city and spend a year there, doing business and making a profit.
verses.kjv: Go to now, ye that say, To day or to morrow we will go into such a city, and continue there a year, and buy and sell, and get gain:

words.gloss: I travel as a merchant, engage in trade

verses.greek: οἵτινες οὐκ ἐπίστασθε ⸀τὸ τῆς αὔριον ⸀ποία ἡ ζωὴ ὑμῶν· ἀτμὶς γάρ ⸀ἐστε ⸀ἡ πρὸς ὀλίγον φαινομένη, ⸀ἔπειτα καὶ ἀφανιζομένη·
verses.mistral: You do not know what tomorrow will bring. You are merely a vapor that appears for a little while and then disappears.
verses.kjv: Whereas ye know not what shall be on the morrow. For what is your life? It is even a vapour, that appeareth for a little time, and then vanisheth away.

words.gloss: I cause to disappear, I hide, remove, disfigure

verses.greek: ἀντὶ τοῦ λέγειν ὑμᾶς· Ἐὰν ὁ κύριος ⸀θελήσῃ, καὶ ⸀ζήσομεν καὶ ⸀ποιήσομεν τοῦτο ἢ ἐκεῖνο.
verses.mistral: Instead of saying to you: If the Lord wills, we shall live and do this or that.
verses.kjv: For that ye ought to say, If the Lord will, we shall live, and do this, or that.

verses.greek: νῦν δὲ καυχᾶσθε ἐν ταῖς ἀλαζονείαις ὑμῶν· πᾶσα καύχησις τοιαύτη πονηρά ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: Now boast in your arrogance; every such boasting is evil.
verses.kjv: But now ye rejoice in your boastings: all such rejoicing is evil.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: εἰδότι οὖν καλὸν ποιεῖν καὶ μὴ ποιοῦντι, ἁμαρτία αὐτῷ ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: Good is to do and not to do is sinning to him.
verses.kjv: Therefore to him that knoweth to do good, and doeth it not, to him it is sin.

verses.greek: Ἄγε νῦν οἱ πλούσιοι, κλαύσατε ὀλολύζοντες ἐπὶ ταῖς ταλαιπωρίαις ὑμῶν ταῖς ἐπερχομέναις.
verses.mistral: Weep now, the rich, weep and wail for your impending troubles.
verses.kjv: Go to now, ye rich men, weep and howl for your miseries that shall come upon you.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ὁ πλοῦτος ὑμῶν σέσηπεν, καὶ τὰ ἱμάτια ὑμῶν σητόβρωτα γέγονεν,
verses.mistral: Your wealth has rotted, and your fine garments have become moth-eaten.
verses.kjv: Your riches are corrupted, and your garments are motheaten.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ὁ χρυσὸς ὑμῶν καὶ ὁ ἄργυρος κατίωται, καὶ ὁ ἰὸς αὐτῶν εἰς μαρτύριον ὑμῖν ἔσται καὶ φάγεται τὰς σάρκας ὑμῶν· ὡς πῦρ ἐθησαυρίσατε ἐν ἐσχάταις ἡμέραις.
verses.mistral: Your gold and silver are corroded, And their rust will be a testimony to you, And will eat your flesh like fire; You have stored up treasure in the last days.
verses.kjv: Your gold and silver is cankered; and the rust of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.

words.gloss: I store up, treasure up, save

verses.greek: ἰδοὺ ὁ μισθὸς τῶν ἐργατῶν τῶν ἀμησάντων τὰς χώρας ὑμῶν ὁ ⸀ἀφυστερημένος ἀφ’ ὑμῶν κράζει, καὶ αἱ βοαὶ τῶν θερισάντων εἰς τὰ ὦτα Κυρίου Σαβαὼθ ⸀εἰσεληλύθασιν·
verses.mistral: The reward of the workers who have not cultivated your lands is crying out, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of Sabath.
verses.kjv: Behold, the hire of the labourers who have reaped down your fields, which is of you kept back by fraud, crieth: and the cries of them which have reaped are entered into the ears of the Lord of sabaoth.

words.gloss: I go in, come in, enter

verses.greek: ἐτρυφήσατε ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς καὶ ἐσπαταλήσατε, ἐθρέψατε τὰς καρδίας ⸀ὑμῶν ἐν ἡμέρᾳ σφαγῆς.
verses.mistral: You have been carried away with the world and have lived luxuriously, you have cultivated your hearts in a day of slaughter.
verses.kjv: Ye have lived in pleasure on the earth, and been wanton; ye have nourished your hearts, as in a day of slaughter.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: κατεδικάσατε, ἐφονεύσατε τὸν δίκαιον. οὐκ ἀντιτάσσεται ὑμῖν;
verses.mistral: You have judged the just, you have killed the righteous. Does it not oppose you?
verses.kjv: Ye have condemned and killed the just; and he doth not resist you.

verses.greek: Μακροθυμήσατε οὖν, ἀδελφοί, ἕως τῆς παρουσίας τοῦ κυρίου. ἰδοὺ ὁ γεωργὸς ἐκδέχεται τὸν τίμιον καρπὸν τῆς γῆς, μακροθυμῶν ἐπ’ ⸀αὐτῷ ἕως ⸀λάβῃ πρόϊμον καὶ ὄψιμον.
verses.mistral: Endure therefore, brethren, until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the farmer waits for the precious produce of the soil, being patient with it until he receives the early and the late rain.
verses.kjv: Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the coming of the Lord. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth, and hath long patience for it, until he receive the early and latter rain.

words.gloss: I suffer long, have patience, am forbearing
I suffer long, have patience, am forbearing
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: μακροθυμήσατε καὶ ὑμεῖς, στηρίξατε τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν, ὅτι ἡ παρουσία τοῦ κυρίου ἤγγικεν.
verses.mistral: Endure therefore, and strengthen your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.
verses.kjv: Be ye also patient; stablish your hearts: for the coming of the Lord draweth nigh.

words.gloss: I suffer long, have patience, am forbearing

verses.greek: μὴ στενάζετε, ⸂ἀδελφοί, κατ’ ἀλλήλων⸃, ἵνα μὴ κριθῆτε· ἰδοὺ ὁ κριτὴς πρὸ τῶν θυρῶν ἕστηκεν.
verses.mistral: Do not grumble, brethren, against one another, that ye may not be judged: behold, the Judge standeth before the doors.
verses.kjv: Grudge not one against another, brethren, lest ye be condemned: behold, the judge standeth before the door.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ὑπόδειγμα λάβετε, ⸀ἀδελφοί, τῆς κακοπαθίας καὶ τῆς μακροθυμίας τοὺς προφήτας, οἳ ἐλάλησαν ⸀ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι κυρίου.
verses.mistral: Take as an example, my brothers, those of prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
verses.kjv: Take, my brethren, the prophets, who have spoken in the name of the Lord, for an example of suffering affliction, and of patience.

verses.greek: ἰδοὺ μακαρίζομεν τοὺς ⸀ὑπομείναντας· τὴν ὑπομονὴν Ἰὼβ ἠκούσατε, καὶ τὸ τέλος κυρίου ⸀εἴδετε, ὅτι πολύσπλαγχνός ἐστιν ⸂ὁ κύριος⸃ καὶ οἰκτίρμων.
verses.mistral: Blessed are those who endure, for they will find favor with the Lord, because He is full of compassion and merciful.
verses.kjv: Behold, we count them happy which endure. Ye have heard of the patience of Job, and have seen the end of the Lord; that the Lord is very pitiful, and of tender mercy.

verses.greek: Πρὸ πάντων δέ, ἀδελφοί μου, μὴ ὀμνύετε, μήτε τὸν οὐρανὸν μήτε τὴν γῆν μήτε ἄλλον τινὰ ὅρκον· ἤτω δὲ ὑμῶν τὸ Ναὶ ναὶ καὶ τὸ Οὒ οὔ, ἵνα μὴ ⸂ὑπὸ κρίσιν⸃ πέσητε.
verses.mistral: Before all things, my brothers, do not swear—neither by heaven nor by earth nor by any other oath. But let your 'Yes' be 'Yes,' and your 'No,' 'No,' so that you may not fall under judgment.
verses.kjv: But above all things, my brethren, swear not, neither by heaven, neither by the earth, neither by any other oath: but let your yea be yea; and your nay, nay; lest ye fall into condemnation.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Κακοπαθεῖ τις ἐν ὑμῖν; προσευχέσθω· εὐθυμεῖ τις; ψαλλέτω.
verses.mistral: Suffers one among you? Let him pray. Rejoices one among you? Let him sing.
verses.kjv: Is any among you afflicted? let him pray. Is any merry? let him sing psalms.

verses.greek: ἀσθενεῖ τις ἐν ὑμῖν; προσκαλεσάσθω τοὺς πρεσβυτέρους τῆς ἐκκλησίας, καὶ προσευξάσθωσαν ἐπ’ αὐτὸν ἀλείψαντες ⸀αὐτὸν ἐλαίῳ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ κυρίου·
verses.mistral: Let him call for the elders of the church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord.
verses.kjv: Is any sick among you? let him call for the elders of the church; and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord:

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ ἡ εὐχὴ τῆς πίστεως σώσει τὸν κάμνοντα, καὶ ἐγερεῖ αὐτὸν ὁ κύριος· κἂν ἁμαρτίας ᾖ πεποιηκώς, ἀφεθήσεται αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: And the prayer of faith shall save the sick, and the Lord shall raise him up; if he has committed sins, he shall be forgiven him.
verses.kjv: And the prayer of faith shall save the sick, and the Lord shall raise him up; and if he have committed sins, they shall be forgiven him.

words.gloss: I send away, release, remit, forgive, permit

verses.greek: ἐξομολογεῖσθε ⸀οὖν ἀλλήλοις ⸂τὰς ἁμαρτίας⸃ καὶ ⸀εὔχεσθε ὑπὲρ ἀλλήλων, ὅπως ἰαθῆτε. πολὺ ἰσχύει δέησις δικαίου ἐνεργουμένη.
verses.mistral: Confess your sins to one another, and pray for each other, so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful in its effect.
verses.kjv: Confess your faults one to another, and pray one for another, that ye may be healed. The effectual fervent prayer of a righteous man availeth much.

verses.greek: Ἠλίας ἄνθρωπος ἦν ὁμοιοπαθὴς ἡμῖν, καὶ προσευχῇ προσηύξατο τοῦ μὴ βρέξαι, καὶ οὐκ ἔβρεξεν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἐνιαυτοὺς τρεῖς καὶ μῆνας ἕξ·
verses.mistral: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain; and it did not rain on the earth for three years and six months.
verses.kjv: Elias was a man subject to like passions as we are, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain: and it rained not on the earth by the space of three years and six months.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ πάλιν προσηύξατο, καὶ ὁ οὐρανὸς ⸂ὑετὸν ἔδωκεν⸃ καὶ ἡ γῆ ἐβλάστησεν τὸν καρπὸν αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: Again he prayed, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
verses.kjv: And he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth brought forth her fruit.

words.gloss: again, further, on the other hand
I sprout, cause to sprout, make to grow up

verses.greek: Ἀδελφοί ⸀μου, ἐάν τις ἐν ὑμῖν πλανηθῇ ἀπὸ τῆς ἀληθείας καὶ ἐπιστρέψῃ τις αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: Return my brethren, if anyone strays from truth and turns back,
verses.kjv: Brethren, if any of you do err from the truth, and one convert him;

verses.greek: ⸀γινωσκέτω ὅτι ὁ ἐπιστρέψας ἁμαρτωλὸν ἐκ πλάνης ὁδοῦ αὐτοῦ σώσει ψυχὴν ⸀αὐτοῦ ἐκ θανάτου καὶ καλύψει πλῆθος ἁμαρτιῶν.
verses.mistral: Saves a soul from death and covers multitudes of sins.
verses.kjv: Let him know, that he which converteth the sinner from the error of his way shall save a soul from death, and shall hide a multitude of sins.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
